entries.title: Mission & Brand Voice
entries.content:
The Family Recovery Foundation (TFRF) is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization founded by Paul Alexander and dedicated to healing families impacted by addiction and preventing substance use. The organization's tagline — "Connected by Recovery. Fortified with Knowledge. United in Faith." — captures its three-part identity: community, education, and Christian faith-based orientation.

TFRF describes itself as a clinically facilitated foundation with a Christian faith-based orientation. Its people are industry experts, professionals, and advocates with decades of combined experience who came together for a common cause. Many team members and leaders share personal stories of addiction and recovery, which grounds the organization's voice in lived experience rather than clinical distance alone.

The brand voice is warm, direct, hopeful, and grounded in real human experience. Key phrases used across the site include:
- "Real people. Real answers. Real hope."
- "Your stories are our stories."
- "No family left behind."
- "We're here every day. If you or someone you love needs help, call us. A real person answers."

TFRF serves families — not just individuals in recovery. Its audience includes parents, spouses, siblings, and loved ones of people struggling with addiction, as well as individuals in recovery themselves. The organization believes families heal as a unit and that generational change is possible when families are equipped with the right tools, knowledge, and support.

The organization's mission is to ensure no one faces addiction alone, delivering free clinical and spiritual care to families across the country through weekly support groups, digital resources, and educational curriculum. Donations are tax-deductible to the fullest extent of the law. Federal Tax I.D. is #87-4108362. The legal entity name for check payments is Lifeline Recovery Services Foundation, DBA The Family Recovery Foundation.

entries.title: Three Pillars: Prevention, Education & Support
entries.content:
The Family Recovery Foundation organizes all of its work around three foundational pillars: Prevention, Education, and Support. These pillars work together so families have the knowledge, skills, and resources to create lasting generational change.

**Pillar 1 — Prevention**
TFRF equips families and communities with evidence-based knowledge and practical tools to keep young people from falling into addiction. Prevention is described as where generational change begins. The organization helps families and communities recognize risk early, build healthy communication, and create environments where young people are less likely to turn to substances as a coping strategy. Prevention delivery methods include early education for families on warning signs and healthy boundaries, community awareness through partnerships with schools and treatment centers, and evidence-based workshops and worksheets that translate research into everyday actions.

**Pillar 2 — Education**
Education turns fear into clarity. TFRF's Intensive Family Program, grief support resources, and expert-led sessions help families understand addiction as a disease, respond with compassion, and stay connected through long-term recovery. Education is delivered through twelve structured modules in the Intensive Family Program, live weekly support sessions, and on-demand learning materials including worksheets and articles.

**Pillar 3 — Support**
TFRF provides direct financial aid for treatment and intervention services, breaking down economic barriers so every family can access the help they deserve. Support includes application-based assistance for residential, outpatient, and detox programs, as well as funding guidance for professional intervention services. The organization's stated belief is that economic circumstances should never be the reason a family cannot pursue recovery support.

entries.title: Family Programming: Weekly Live Sessions
entries.content:
The Family Recovery Foundation offers a robust schedule of free, recurring live programs designed to help families navigate addiction with understanding and compassion. All sessions are led by experienced facilitators and certified counselors.

**Fix Your Family**
Held every Monday at 7:00 PM CT / 5:00 PM PT, this structured weekly session is led by Beth Durling, MS, CADCII, ICADC. Families learn communication strategies, boundary-setting techniques, and conflict resolution skills to rebuild trust and connection. It is described as TFRF's national signature program.

**The Family Room**
Held every Friday at 10:00 AM CT / 8:00 AM PT, this open-format support circle allows family members to share experiences, ask questions, and receive guidance from peer advocates who have walked the same journey of recovery.

**Morning Meditation**
Offered Monday through Friday at 9:00 AM CT / 7:00 AM PT, this 15-minute guided mindfulness practice is designed to reduce anxiety, improve emotional regulation, and build resilience for families navigating recovery together.

**The Courage to Detach**
Held on the last Wednesday of every month at 5:00 PM CT / 3:00 PM PT, this monthly intensive workshop focuses on healthy detachment — learning to love without enabling, set boundaries with compassion, and protect one's own wellbeing while supporting a loved one.

**1-on-1 Family Coaching**
Available by appointment, these personalized coaching sessions are tailored to each family's unique situation. Participants work directly with a certified counselor to develop a customized recovery plan.

**Private Family Messaging Group**
Available 24/7, this secure private messaging community connects families directly with TFRF's Clinical Director and trained peer support team for real-time guidance at any hour.

**Family Education & Resources**
Available on demand, this library includes expert-curated guides on addiction science, family dynamics, intervention strategies, and long-term recovery planning.

entries.title: Intensive Family Program: 12-Module Curriculum
entries.content:
The Family Recovery Foundation offers an Intensive Family Program consisting of twelve structured educational modules designed to help families understand addiction and navigate the recovery journey with compassion and clarity. The modules are available for download and are complemented by live weekly sessions.

The twelve modules cover the following topics:
- Module 1: Definition of Addiction
- Module 2: Our Central Nervous System
- Module 3: Codependency I — Characteristics and Strategies
- Module 4: Codependency II — Manipulation and Enabling
- Module 5: Codependency III — The Neuroscience
- Module 6: Communication Skills — Difficult Conversations
- Module 7: Boundaries — Who I Am and Who I Am Not
- Module 8: The Language of Connective Boundaries
- Module 9: Understanding and Navigating Gaslighting
- Module 10: I Am Coming Home — Navigating the Transition When Your Loved One Comes Home from Treatment
- Module 11: When the Addict Hits THE WALL
- Module 12: Just For Today

In addition to the twelve modules, two downloadable worksheets are available:
- Worksheet 1: Relapse Prevention Plan
- Worksheet 2: Family Support Plan

The curriculum integrates addiction science, codependency education, boundary-setting, communication skills, and practical transition planning. It is designed to be accessible to families at every stage of the recovery journey, whether a loved one is still in active addiction, entering treatment, or returning home from a program.

entries.title: Financial Aid for Treatment & Intervention
entries.content:
The Family Recovery Foundation provides direct financial assistance to help families access quality addiction treatment and intervention services when cost would otherwise stand in the way. The organization's stated belief is that economic circumstances should never be the reason a family cannot pursue recovery support.

Financial aid is delivered through three primary channels:

- **Treatment financial aid:** Application-based assistance for residential, outpatient, and detox programs aligned with each family's clinical needs.
- **Intervention support:** Funding guidance for professional intervention services when a loved one is not yet ready to accept treatment.
- **Scholarship and reduced-fee access:** In 2025, TFRF placed 41 individuals into treatment or intervention services through scholarships or reduced-fee access.

As of the 2025 impact report, the organization has provided $1.2 million in financial aid. The program operates on an application basis, and families can apply through the Get Help page on the TFRF website or by contacting the team directly.

The financial aid program is funded through donations, fundraising events such as the Annual Gala, and campaigns such as the Light the Way holiday matching campaign. TFRF is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit, and all donations are tax-deductible to the fullest extent of the law.

entries.title: 2025 Impact Report & Survey Results
entries.content:
The Family Recovery Foundation's 2025 impact data reflects significant reach and measurable outcomes for families navigating addiction and recovery. The following statistics are drawn from the organization's 2025 survey results and annual impact report.

**Program Reach in 2025:**
- 1,732 families served through the Fix Your Family program
- 1,100 participants engaged in Morning Meditation support
- 421 hours of one-on-one coaching provided at no cost since September
- 70 family crisis interventions delivered
- 90 families supported through The Family Room
- 41 individuals placed into treatment or intervention services through scholarships or reduced-fee access

**Cumulative Impact Metrics:**
- 1,720+ families supported globally
- $1.2 million in financial aid provided
- 23 states reached
- 24/7 private messaging access available

**2025 Survey Satisfaction Results:**
- 92% of respondents said Fix Your Family was very helpful
- 89% said Morning Meditation helped them stay emotionally grounded
- 96% said one-on-one coaching helped them understand their family dynamics
- 96% said the private chat helped them feel less alone and more emotionally grounded
- 100% of survey respondents said they would recommend TFRF to another family in need
- 100% said registration, communication, and scheduling were easy
- 87% said their family communication improved since starting TFRF programs
- 83% said TFRF helped them feel more emotionally stable
- 78% said they understand addiction and mental health more clearly

entries.title: Leadership: Board of Directors
entries.content:
The Family Recovery Foundation is governed by a Board of Directors composed of experienced professionals in behavioral health, business, and recovery advocacy.

**Paul Alexander — Founder**
Paul Alexander is the founder of The Family Recovery Foundation. His personal story is featured in the organization's video testimonials.

**Devon Wayt — Vice President**
Devon Wayt is a person in long-term recovery with more than 23 years of experience across all populations and levels of care in behavioral health treatment and recovery-oriented systems of care. He earned a Bachelor of Science in management and a Master of Business Administration from Pepperdine University's Graziadio School of Business and Management. Devon is the Founder and CEO of Circa Behavioral Healthcare Solutions, and Co-Founder of Mint Billing and Como HR. He serves on the CCAPP Credentialing Board, as Chair of the California Alliance for Behavioral Health and Addiction Treatment (CABAT), and on the Nowell Family Foundation Board.

**Andrew Engbring — CEO & Founder, Reflection Family Interventions**
Andrew began his career in 2013 managing a sober living home in Encinitas, California. He co-founded Reflection Family Interventions with his wife, emphasizing that families are central to successful recovery. He is trained in intervention, psychology, and family systems and is an Eagle Scout.

**Ilana Zivkovich — Founder and CEO of Werq**
Ilana is a Certified Executive Coach and LCSW with a Master's degree in Social Work. She previously served as a C-Suite leader of behavioral healthcare organizations. She is a member of WPO (Women's Presidents Organization) and serves on the Board of VentureLab.

**Mike S. Early, CSAC-WI (Retired)**
Mike has devoted over 50 years to addiction treatment and recovery. He held senior roles at the Hazelden Foundation and Caron Treatment Centers, where he served as EVP/Chief Clinical Officer. He served as President of the WI Alcohol and Drug Counselor Certification Board and Chair of the IC&RC's Case Presentation Method Committee. His personal recovery story was featured in Moments of Clarity by Christopher Kennedy Lawford.

entries.title: Leadership: Staff
entries.content:
The Family Recovery Foundation's staff includes nationally recognized clinicians and advocates who bring deep professional and personal experience to the organization's mission.

**Beth Durling, MS, CADCII, ICADC — Executive Director**
Beth Durling is a nationally recognized clinician, educator, and consultant with over 20 years of experience in the behavioral health field. She holds a Master's degree in Psychology and is certified in addiction counseling at both the state and international levels. Her clinical approach integrates Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, Inner Child healing, family systems theory, spirituality, and trauma recovery.

Beth serves as Executive Director of The Family Recovery Foundation and is the creator of Fix Your Family, the organization's national signature program. She has previously served as CEO, COO, and Clinical Director in behavioral health settings, and founded The Center for Life Change, a nationally respected nonprofit treatment center. Alongside her nonprofit work, Beth leads The Agency, a behavioral health consulting firm. She lives in San Clemente, California.

**Ashley Drake, MA — Chief Marketing & Development Officer**
Ashley Drake holds a Master of Arts degree and serves as Chief Marketing & Development Officer. Her motivation to help others is rooted in her own personal recovery journey, which began in 2010. Her passion is to aid in the recovery process of those suffering from addiction as well as their loved ones.

entries.title: Oklahoma City Annual Gala 2026
entries.content:
The Family Recovery Foundation's Annual Gala in Oklahoma City is one of the organization's primary fundraising events, bringing together community leaders, advocates, and partners who believe families impacted by addiction and mental health challenges deserve access to support, guidance, and hope at no cost.

**Event Details:**
- Date: Thursday, June 11, 2026
- Doors Open: 6:00 PM
- Venue: Newchurch, 9201 N Rockwell Ave, Oklahoma City, OK 73132

**Evening Schedule:**
- 6:00 PM — Doors open; welcome reception with hors d'oeuvres and refreshments
- 6:30 PM — Dinner service
- 7:30 PM — Mission Moment featuring stories from families impacted by TFRF programs
- 8:00 PM — Live auction and fundraising appeal
- 9:00 PM — Entertainment and dancing

**Ticket Pricing:**
- Individual ticket (general admission): $65
- Table of 8 (reserved seating): $500

**Sponsorship Tiers:**
- Silver Sponsor: $1,000 — logo on event program and website, social media recognition, 2 complimentary tickets, acknowledgement during event
- Gold Sponsor: $1,500 — all Silver benefits plus logo on signage, 4 tickets, table placement priority, pre-event communications feature
- Platinum Sponsor: $2,500 — all Gold benefits plus prominent logo on all materials, 8 tickets, reserved premium table, speaking opportunity, post-event recognition video
- Legacy Sponsor: $5,000 — all Platinum benefits plus title sponsorship, 10 tickets, private reception with leadership, year-long website feature, exclusive partnership branding, custom recognition package

All proceeds support families affected by addiction and mental health challenges.

entries.title: Nashville Annual Gala 2026
entries.content:
The Family Recovery Foundation's Nashville Gala is the organization's second annual fundraising gala event, held in Music City to expand its reach and mission across the southeastern United States.

**Event Details:**
- Date: Thursday, November 5, 2026
- Doors Open: 5:30 PM
- Venue: Copperline Ranch, 1 Copperline Rd, Heritage, TN 37076
- Dinner served at 6:00 PM

**Ticket Pricing:**
- Individual ticket (general admission): $85
- Table of 8 (reserved seating): $650

**Sponsorship Tiers:**
- Silver Sponsor: $1,500 — name or logo in event program, social media thank-you post, 2 complimentary tickets, recognition on gala sponsor slide
- Gold Sponsor: $5,000 — small logo on gala signage, verbal acknowledgment during opening remarks, 4 complimentary tickets, 2 social media highlights
- Platinum Sponsor: $7,500 — medium logo on gala signage, half-page ad or tribute in gala program, 6 complimentary tickets, sponsor feature on social media with company bio and link
- Legacy Sponsor: $10,000 — premier logo placement on all printed materials, full-page ad or tribute in gala program, 8 complimentary tickets with VIP table seating, exclusive on-stage thank you

The purpose of both galas is to grow and sustain TFRF's mission, ensuring families impacted by addiction and mental health challenges can access support, guidance, and hope at no cost. All proceeds support families affected by addiction and mental health challenges.

entries.title: Light the Way: Holiday Matching Campaign
entries.content:
Light the Way is The Family Recovery Foundation's holiday fundraising campaign, structured as a matching gift initiative to maximize donor impact during the holiday season.

The campaign features a $5,000 matching fund, meaning that donations made during the campaign period are matched dollar-for-dollar, doubling the impact of each gift. The campaign is designed to help families access prevention, education, and support when they need it most.

Donations made through the Light the Way campaign support TFRF's core programs, including financial aid for treatment and intervention services, free family programming, and educational resources. The campaign messaging emphasizes that every gift lights the path toward healing for someone walking the recovery journey today.

TFRF is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization with Federal Tax I.D. #87-4108362. All donations are tax-deductible to the fullest extent of the law. Checks should be made payable to "Lifeline Recovery Services Foundation" with a note "DBA The Family Recovery Foundation" and mailed to: Lifeline Recovery Services Foundation, 9842 13th St, Garden Grove CA 92844.

The organization also accepts online donations through its website at tfrfoundation.org/donate.

entries.title: Get Help: How Families Can Access Support
entries.content:
The Family Recovery Foundation makes it straightforward for families to reach out and begin receiving support. The organization emphasizes that a real person answers every call and that help is available every day.

**Primary Contact Methods:**
- Phone: 888-964-8825 (answered every day by a real person)
- Email: info@tfrfoundation.org
- Online contact form: available at tfrfoundation.org/get-help and tfrfoundation.org/contact

The Get Help form, introduced by Executive Director Beth Durling, MS, CADCII, ICADC, collects basic contact information and asks whether the person is seeking help for themselves or a loved one. The form is not HIPAA compliant, and TFRF discloses this clearly to users before submission.

**Ways to Access Programs:**
- Register for weekly live sessions (Fix Your Family, The Family Room, Morning Meditation, The Courage to Detach) through the Family Programming page
- Apply for financial aid for treatment and intervention services through the Get Help page
- Access on-demand educational modules and downloadable worksheets at any time
- Join the Private Family Messaging Group with the Clinical Director for 24/7 support
- Schedule a 1-on-1 Family Coaching session by appointment

After registering, participants receive a confirmation email with session times and links to join. TFRF also maintains a presence on Instagram at @tfr_foundation, where a private family group is available to registered participants.

entries.title: Trusted National Resources for Families
entries.content:
The Family Recovery Foundation curates a list of trusted national organizations that provide research, support, and treatment information for families navigating addiction and mental health challenges. These external resources complement TFRF's own programming and are available through the Resources page at tfrfoundation.org/resources.

The following national organizations are listed as trusted resources:

- **DRUGABUSE.GOV** — National Institute on Drug Abuse: research, education, and treatment resources
- **DRUGFREE.ORG** — Partnership to End Addiction: tools and support for families
- **SAMHSA.GOV** — Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration: national helpline and treatment locator
- **NIAAA.NIH.GOV** — National Institute on Alcohol Abuse and Alcoholism: research and resources
- **HEALTHCHILDREN.ORG** — American Academy of Pediatrics: information for parents on child health and substance use
- **CDC.ORG** — Centers for Disease Control and Prevention: data, statistics, and prevention strategies

In addition to these national resources, TFRF maintains a network of partner treatment centers and community organizations. Families can also access downloadable worksheets — including a Relapse Prevention Plan and a Family Support Plan — directly from the TFRF website at tfrfoundation.org/worksheets.

entries.title: Partnership Network: Treatment Centers & Community Organizations
entries.content:
The Family Recovery Foundation has built a network of partner treatment centers and community organizations that share its mission of healing families and preventing substance use. Partners collaborate on programming, events, and resource sharing to expand access to care, education, and hope.

Partnership tiers include Platinum Partner, Silver Partner, and Community Champion.

**Platinum Partner:**
- Northbound Treatment Center — a leader in addiction recovery offering personalized care and evidence-based programs for individuals and families

**Silver Partner:**
- La Ventana Treatment Center — provides compassionate and comprehensive mental health and substance abuse recovery with highly individualized care

**Community Champions include:**
- Restore Health and Wellness Center
- Trauma and Beyond Psychological Center
- Purpose Health
- The Canyon at Santa Monica
- The Retreat
- Reflection Family Interventions
- Pathways Recovery Centers
- Rob's Ranch
- Recovery Beach (Garden Grove, California)
- Hart Recovery (Tulsa, Oklahoma — sober living for women)
- Apex Recovery (Tennessee)
- Tunnel Vision Recovery
- Clear Path Intervention
- Alliance Housing
- CarePossible (serving veterans, military families, and low-income families)
- Dr. Randy Moraitis (Certified Intervention Professional)
- Plugged In Recovery (Scottsdale and Chandler, Arizona)
- Into Action Recovery Centers
- Change Your Brain Foundation
- Finding Hope

Treatment centers and community organizations interested in partnering with TFRF can reach out through the organization's contact page.

entries.title: Get Involved: Volunteering, Fundraising & Contributing
entries.content:
The Family Recovery Foundation offers multiple pathways for individuals and organizations to get involved and support its mission of healing families impacted by addiction.

**Ways to Get Involved:**

- **Donate:** Financial contributions fund vital programs and resources aimed at prevention, education, and family support. Donations are tax-deductible.
- **Share your story:** Individuals can contribute to the TFRF blog or share personal experiences to raise awareness and inspire other families on the recovery journey.
- **Volunteer:** Volunteers can join a committee, offer professional services, or provide spiritual support through prayer. The organization also welcomes help with photography, videography, and other professional services.
- **Fundraise:** Supporters can organize or participate in fundraising events to mobilize resources for families impacted by addiction.
- **Partner with TFRF:** Treatment centers and community organizations can collaborate on programming, events, and resource sharing.
- **Connect a family:** Supporters can share TFRF's resources with someone who needs help or introduce the organization to their community.

The Get Involved form at tfrfoundation.org/form-make-a-difference allows interested individuals to indicate how they would like to help, with options including blog contribution, volunteer/committee work, fundraising, professional services, and other support.

The organization also invites supporters to join The Family Recovery Foundation Newsletter to stay connected with the latest news and information.

entries.title: Testimonials & Family Stories
entries.content:
The Family Recovery Foundation collects and shares written and video testimonials from families and individuals whose lives have been touched by its programs. These stories are featured on the Testimonials page at tfrfoundation.org/about/testimonials and the Stories page at tfrfoundation.org/stories.

Video stories include perspectives from:
- Ashley — whose husband was affected by the disease of addiction
- Paul — Founder of The Family Recovery Foundation
- Jillian — Board Member of The Family Recovery Foundation
- Alexa — affected by the disease of addiction
- Jason — in recovery and family affected by addiction
- Jodi — a mother who lost a child to an overdose
- Mitch — a father who lost a child to an overdose
- Justin — family affected by the disease of addiction
- Alex — friends affected by the disease of addiction
- Angela — child of an adult alcoholic

Written testimonials highlight themes of boundary-setting, understanding addiction as a disease, improved family communication, reduced shame, and the value of community. One testimonial from Karen in Chicago describes Fix Your Family as helping her navigate "the complex world of addiction and mental health" and teaching tools for setting boundaries with compassion. A testimonial from Oregon describes the weekly family sessions and morning meditations as providing "tools, strength, and hope" during one of the hardest seasons of a family's life.

TFRF's 2025 survey found that 100% of respondents would recommend the organization to another family in need.

entries.title: Contact Information & Office Locations
entries.content:
The Family Recovery Foundation maintains multiple office locations across the United States and is reachable by phone and email every day of the week.

**Primary Contact:**
- Phone: 888-964-8825
- Email: info@tfrfoundation.org
- Website: tfrfoundation.org
- Instagram: @tfr_foundation

The organization emphasizes that a real person answers the phone every day: "We're here every day. If you or someone you love needs help, call us. A real person answers."

**Office Locations:**

- **Newport Beach, CA:** 4701 Teller Ave Suite 150E, 92660
- **Seattle, WA:** 2120 1st Ave. N #313, 98109
- **Phoenix, AZ:** 3838 N Central Ave. Ste 956, 85012
- **Goldsby, OK:** 266 Industrial Blvd, 73093

**Mailing Address for Donations (checks):**
Lifeline Recovery Services Foundation
9842 13th St
Garden Grove, CA 92844
(Make checks payable to "Lifeline Recovery Services Foundation" with note "DBA The Family Recovery Foundation")

Families seeking help can also submit an online form at tfrfoundation.org/get-help or tfrfoundation.org/contact. The form collects basic contact information and whether the person is seeking help for themselves or a loved one. TFRF notes that the contact form is not HIPAA compliant and that information is transmitted via encrypted but non-HIPAA-compliant email.

entries.title: Blog & Educational Content Hub
entries.content:
The Family Recovery Foundation maintains a blog and educational content hub called The Family Recovery Foundation Journal, described as offering "thoughtful guidance for healing at home." The blog is accessible at tfrfoundation.org/blog and covers topics related to mental health, addiction recovery, and family-centered resources.

Content is produced by The Family Recovery Foundation team and is positioned as a resource for families navigating addiction at every stage of the journey. The blog complements the organization's live programming and on-demand educational modules.

In addition to the blog, TFRF offers:
- On-demand educational modules from the Intensive Family Program (12 modules available for download)
- Downloadable worksheets including a Relapse Prevention Plan and a Family Support Plan (available at tfrfoundation.org/worksheets)
- A curated list of trusted national resources from organizations such as SAMHSA, NIDA, the CDC, and the American Academy of Pediatrics
- Video content available on YouTube, including recordings of group sessions

The organization invites community members to contribute to the blog by sharing insights, experiences, and perspectives to raise awareness and inspire others. Those interested in contributing can submit a Get Involved form at tfrfoundation.org/form-make-a-difference.

The blog and resource library are designed to be accessible anytime, anywhere, supporting families between live sessions and providing entry points for those not yet ready to join a live program.
